item.title: Ed Miliband calls for UK to cut greenhouse gas emissions by 87% by 2040
item.description: Energy secretary’s proposed legally binding target sets up battle with opposition parties over ‘net zero’ policies

item.title: Adnoc plans new UAE pipeline to bypass Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Move by state-run oil group comes as Iran war highlights region’s reliance on key shipping route

item.title: Anthropic to expand Mythos access to more than 15 countries
item.description: About 150 organisations will be given advanced cyber security model following requests from around the world

item.title: UK government pushes ahead with zero-hours crackdown despite business warnings
item.description: New right intended to fulfil Labour’s pre-election pledge to ban ‘exploitative’ contracts

item.title: Events group Hyve sold to private equity firm Hellman & Friedman for $1.8bn
item.description: Conference and trade show organiser benefiting from people wishing to meet in person again

item.title: The Proxies 2026: A celebration of substantial corporate perks
item.description: When salary isn’t enough

item.title: Castlelake to hunt partner on easyJet bid to navigate EU ownership rules
item.description: US private credit group would be bound by rules requiring airline to be majority owned by EU nationals

item.title: Oil industry faces ‘gruesome’ demand shock from Iran crisis
item.description: The prospects for a rapid and full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz look bleak

item.title: Google’s $80bn equity raise adds to that giant AI sucking sound
item.description: Search engine’s move is a good example of how artificial intelligence has made big numbers all but meaningless

item.title: US boosts nuclear fuel supply chain as Russia import ban looms
item.description: Urenco announces multibillion-dollar investment to build second uranium enrichment plant in New Mexico

item.title: China’s hunt for US tungsten escalates global critical minerals race
item.description: Effort has driven up prices and prompted calls to restrict sales of a vital national security resource to overseas buyers

item.title: Scottish EV start-up plans new UK plant in 2027
item.description: Munro is to build a new facility for industrial-use electric vehicles

item.title: British Land picks Canadian pension fund executive as next boss
item.description: Joanne McNamara will become one of a select group of women running FTSE 100 groups

item.title: IPO boom, market doom?
item.description: Plus: not now, Godzilla

item.title: This will be an absolutely mammoth year for IPOs. Probably
item.description: Partying like it’s 1999

item.title: Activist fund Elliott calls for sale of Australia’s largest gold miner
item.description: US firm builds up stake in Northern Star Resources

item.title: FirstFT: US in talks to widen nuclear arms deployments in Europe
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: BP chair search and Macron’s fading legacy

item.title: EasyJet’s summer sale reaches Wall Street
item.description: Europe’s famed budget airline has become a potential takeover target as war in Iran hits industry

item.title: Castlelake: the private credit lender taking a run at easyJet
item.description: The $37bn investment manager is little known in the UK, but that could change if it bids on the British budget carrier

item.title: Greek tanker operator Dynacom readies vessels for possible opening of Hormuz
item.description: The group, one of the few to transit the strait since the conflict, has moved six ships in the vicinity

item.title: US start-up plans to drill for lithium under VW and BMW battery factories
item.description: Atana Elements acquires exploration licences in Germany and Poland as Europe aims to reduce reliance on China

item.title: Deutsche Bank’s comeback
item.description: After decades of upheaval, the lender that wanted to take on Wall Street has scaled back its ambitions to focus on Europe

item.title: Will the IT consulting share price rout ever end?
item.description: Accenture made a fortune from previous tech revolutions but investors think AI could kill it, not make it stronger

item.title: Top AI labs expand research into machine ‘consciousness’
item.description: Google DeepMind, Anthropic and Meta study whether AI could become conscious — and what follows for humans if it does

item.title: India Inc gears up for opening of nuclear power to private sector
item.description: Tata Power and Adani Group among conglomerates seeking approval for plant construction

item.title: General Mills sells Häagen-Dazs ice cream stores in China
item.description: Transaction is the latest in shift towards local management for well-known foreign brands

item.title: Tencent moves closer to launching AI agent for China’s most-used app
item.description: Maker of WeChat has fallen behind domestic rivals in artificial intelligence models

item.title: Florida sues OpenAI and Altman for ‘hurting’ children
item.description: State files lawsuit over ‘litany of harms’ it claims the company’s chatbots have caused

item.title: China’s housing market could have further to fall
item.description: To make real estate attractive for investors, prices must go lower

item.title: Aspiration Partners’ co-founder Joe Sanberg gets 14-year fraud sentence
item.description: Case has thrown spotlight on dealings with Wall Street and Hollywood elites including billionaire Steve Ballmer
